_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Not responding to IV Antibiotic therapy even after 72 hours
Abscess is painful and presenting as large swelling on P/V examination, proceed for colpotomy

clinical_thresholds.note: after 72 hours of IV antibiotic therapy

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes including history symptoms, signs, examination findings, planned line of treatment, and admission advice

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history including previous surgical history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission?
Did clinical examination and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the clinical examination and imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Size of abscess in ultrasound report did not necessitate the drainage procedure or not documented.
History of recent surgical procedure followed by signs of infection not documented or inconsistent.
Detailed clinical notes for pre-authorization are missing or incomplete.
USG abdomen/pelvis report not submitted or does not confirm the diagnosis.
Operative/procedure notes are missing or lack sufficient detail on surgical steps and findings.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Well-equipped facility for colpotomy, when indicated
clinical_key_pointers: A pelvic abscess is a life-threatening collection of infected fluid in the pouch of Douglas, fallopian tube, ovary, or parametric tissue.
Usually, a pelvic abscess occurs as a complication after operative procedures such as septic abortion, abdominal or vaginal procedures.
It starts as pelvic cellulitis or hematoma spreads to parametrial tissue.

It can also present as a result of the complexity of certain medical conditions like sexually transmitted infection, pelvic inflammatory disease, appendicitis, diverticulitis, inflammatory bowel disease.
